task: Design and draft a showcase section for my personal academic homepage. Please first generate the HTML/content structure and placeholder layout, then clearly list what images/assets are needed for each showcase block, and finally organize everything into a clean insertion-ready package so I can manually add the final images later.
goal: Create a polished homepage showcase section that presents my research trajectory in a clear and visually compelling way for future academic job applications.

style_preferences: make the section easy for me to update later

logic: Block 1 should emphasize atlas-scale single-cell work
Block 2 should emphasize pancreatic cancer ecosystem heterogeneity and the PANCOSMOS resource
Block 3 should emphasize mechanism-oriented or translational work, so the page does not look like it only contains descriptive atlases
showcase_strategy.rationale: The section should communicate that I can build atlases, interpret ecosystem structure, and generate mechanistic or translational insights.

blocks.title: Tumor Vascular Atlas
blocks.subtitle: Single-cell dissection of tumor vascular heterogeneity and angiogenic programs.
blocks.purpose: This block should showcase my vascular atlas work and establish me as someone capable of building high-resolution single-cell maps with biological interpretation.

text: Write a concise title and 2–4 sentence summary.
Mention tumor vascular heterogeneity and angiogenesis.
Keep wording accessible for a broad academic audience.
visual_layout: Use two visual slots side by side or stacked depending on page width.
Visual 1: a representative single-cell atlas figure.
Visual 2: a schematic or conceptual illustration of tumor angiogenesis / vascular remodeling.

image_assets_needed.name: vascular_single_cell_atlas
image_assets_needed.description: A clean representative figure showing the vascular single-cell atlas.

image_assets_needed.name: angiogenesis_schematic
image_assets_needed.description: A schematic or conceptual model illustrating tumor angiogenesis or vascular evolution.

blocks.title: PDAC Ecosystem Heterogeneity
blocks.subtitle: Large-scale single-cell and spatial dissection of pancreatic cancer microenvironmental diversity.
blocks.purpose: This block should present my pancreatic cancer ecosystem work as a flagship systems-biology study and also highlight PANCOSMOS as a field-facing resource.

text: Write a concise title and 2–4 sentence summary.
Emphasize ecosystem heterogeneity rather than only cell annotation.
Mention integration of single-cell and spatial transcriptomic analyses.
Include a short introduction to PANCOSMOS as an interactive resource.
visual_layout: Use one main scientific image plus a smaller linked PANCOSMOS logo/resource card.
Main visual: one representative figure for PDAC ecosystem heterogeneity.
Secondary visual: PANCOSMOS logo or branded resource tile with link.

image_assets_needed.name: pdac_ecosystem_main_figure
image_assets_needed.description: A representative figure illustrating PDAC ecosystem heterogeneity.

image_assets_needed.name: pancosmos_logo
image_assets_needed.description: Logo or clean badge for PANCOSMOS with external link.
preferred_content: transparent PNG or SVG if possible

blocks.title: Mechanism and Translational Insights
blocks.subtitle: From spatially resolved biology to clinically relevant hypotheses.
blocks.purpose: This block should prevent the homepage from looking purely descriptive. It should show that I can move from atlas-scale observation toward mechanism or translational relevance.
preferred_direction: or epigenomic / biomarker / translational multi-omics work if that fits the page better

text: Write a concise title and 2–4 sentence summary.
Frame the work as mechanism-oriented or translation-oriented.
Show progression from discovery to biological or clinical significance.
visual_layout: Prefer a mechanism model, spatial validation figure, or translational workflow graphic.

image_assets_needed.name: mechanism_or_translation_figure
image_assets_needed.description: A representative figure for the third block.

implementation_instructions.step_1: Draft the showcase section in HTML first, with polished text, placeholders for images, and consistent card-based or section-based layout.
implementation_instructions.step_2: Use placeholder image filenames and comments so I can easily replace them later.
implementation_instructions.step_3: After drafting the section, produce a separate checklist of all required images/assets I need to prepare.
implementation_instructions.step_4: Then produce a final organized deliverable containing: (1) the HTML block, (2) suggested CSS if needed, and (3) the asset checklist.
implementation_instructions.step_5: Keep all text editable and modular so I can update titles, summaries, and links later.

layout_preference: cards or alternating sections both acceptable
do_not_do: do not overload with too many projects
do not make it look like a publication dump
do not require final images before drafting the structure

final_request_to_claude: Please first write the showcase section structure and polished text using placeholders, then tell me exactly which images/assets you need from me, and finally organize everything into a clean insertion-ready output so I can manually replace the placeholders later.
